back_translation: All you brothers and sisters, I do not want you to be without knowledge in the matter that all of our forefathers were under the cloud, and they all crossed through the sea.
And all of them received baptism into Moses, in the cloud and in the sea.
And all of them ate the same spiritual food.
And all of them drank the same spiritual drink, because they drank from the spiritual rock that followed them; and that rock was Christ.
But God was not pleased with the majority of them — because they were struck-down-and-died in the wilderness.
These events became examples for us, so that we would not be people who crave evil things, just as those people craved.
Do not be people who worship idols, like some of them. According to what is written: "The people sat down to eat and drink, then rose up to dance-and-revel."
Let us not commit sexual immorality, like some of them committed sexual immorality, and in one day twenty-three thousand people fell-dead.
Let us not put Christ to the test, like some of them tested-Him, and they were destroyed by the snakes.
And do not grumble-mutter, like some of them grumbled-muttered, and they were destroyed by the destroying angel.
These things happened to them as types-and-examples, and have been written for our admonition — for us upon whom the end-of-the-ages has come.
Therefore, the one who thinks that he himself is standing firm, let him be careful lest he fall.

The temptation that has come to you all is nothing other than what is common to mankind. God is faithful (royal-form); He will not (royally) allow you to be tested beyond what you can bear; but at the time of the testing, He will (royally) provide a way out as well, so that you may be able to endure.
Therefore, my beloved ones, flee from idol-worship.
I am speaking to you as people of wisdom; you yourselves please consider what I say.
The cup of blessing which we bless — is it not the participation in the (royal) blood of Christ? The bread that we break — is it not the participation in the (royal) body of Christ?
Because there is one loaf, we are therefore one body — although we are many — because we all receive a share from that one loaf.
Consider Israel according to the flesh: are not those who eat the sacrifices also participants in the altar?
If so, what am I now saying? That food offered to idols is anything? Or that an idol is anything?
Not at all; but the things that the Gentiles sacrifice, they sacrifice to demons, not to God. I do not want you all to become participants with demons.
You all cannot drink the cup of the (royal) Lord and the cup of demons at the same time. You all cannot have a share in the table of the (royal) Lord and the table of demons at the same time.
Or are we causing the Lord to be (royally) jealous? Are we stronger than Him?
"One can do anything at all" — but not everything is beneficial. "One can do anything at all" — but not everything builds up.
Let no one seek his own benefit, but rather the benefit of the others.
All food that is sold in the meat-market — go ahead and eat it, without inquiring about anything for the sake of conscience —
because "the earth and everything filling the earth belongs to the (royal) Lord."
If anyone among the unbelievers invites you all, and you all desire to go, then go ahead and eat all that he sets before you, without inquiring about anything for the sake of conscience.
But if anyone says to you all, "This is offered to deities," then do not eat — for the sake of the one who told you, and for the sake of conscience —
the conscience that I refer to is not your own, but is the other one's. Why should my freedom be judged by another person's conscience?
If I myself partake with grace and thanksgiving, why am I criticized concerning that for which I have given thanks?
Therefore, whether you all eat or drink, or do whatever it is — do all things for the (royal) honor of God.
Do not be cause-of-stumbling, whether toward Jews, or toward Greeks, or toward the church of God —
just as I myself try to please all people in all things, not seeking my own benefit, but the benefit of the many people, in order that they may receive salvation.
